events.text: Kenneth Flores, 60-year-old Black male, Former smoker (quit 5 years ago). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Malignant melanoma of the Left upper back. Clinical staging: T4bN2aM1c, AJCC Stage IV. ICD-10: C43.9. Stage IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation, lung and brain metastases.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Underwent primary resection: Wide local excision with 2 cm margins and sentinel lymph node biopsy. Estimated blood loss 400 mL. Length of stay 5 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated first-line BRAF/MEK-directed therapy: Dabrafenib 150 mg BID + Trametinib 2 mg daily. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on first-line BRAF/MEK-directed therapy.
Clinical visit for follow-up on first-line BRAF/MEK-directed therapy.
Completed brain metastases treatment: Stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) 20 Gy single fraction to 2 brain metastases. Treatment delivered as planned.
Clinical visit for post-radiation assessment.
Initiated alternative first-line immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 3 pyrexia. Blood cultures drawn. Empiric antibiotics held as cultures negative. Managed with acetaminophen.
Initiated alternative first-line immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
Initiated alternative first-line immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 diarrhea. Managed with loperamide 4 mg then 2 mg after each loose stool. Stool studies negative.
Initiated alternative first-line immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 2 rash. Treated with topical triamcinolone 0.1% cream BID and oral cetirizine 10 mg daily.
Initiated alternative first-line immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after alternative first-line immunotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after alternative first-line immunotherapy.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
BRAIN MRI WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Kenneth Flores
MRN: patient_22786d702301
Date of Study: 2022-02-28

CLINICAL INDICATION: Malignant melanoma of the left upper back. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: MRI performed with and without IV gadolinium contrast, multiplanar multisequence technique.

FINDINGS:
BRAIN: 2 enhancing lesions identified: left occipital lobe (2.2 cm), right frontal lobe (2.1 cm). Surrounding vasogenic edema. No midline shift.

IMPRESSION:
1. Brain metastases as described. Recommend neurosurgical consultation.
2. Pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease.

Electronically signed by: David Kim, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Kenneth Flores
MRN: patient_22786d702301
Specimen ID: S2022-21504
Date of Procedure: 2022-03-03
Date of Report: 2022-03-06

SPECIMEN: Left upper back, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Malignant melanoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 5.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
S-100: Positive
HMB-45: Positive
Melan-A: Positive
SOX10: Positive
Ki-67: 30%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Kenneth Flores - Left upper back" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 5.2 x 3.6 x 2.6 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A9.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Dermatology

Patient: Kenneth Flores
MRN: patient_22786d702301
Date: 2022-03-10
Provider: Dr. Priya Sharma

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Flores is a 60-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed malignant melanoma of the left upper back, IV. Patient was referred by primary care physician after abnormal imaging. Stage IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation, lung and brain metastases. Pathology confirmed malignant melanoma. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports incidental finding on routine screening.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- atrial fibrillation

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- No prior surgeries

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 2 years ago)
- Alcohol: Social drinker (2-3 drinks/week)
- Occupation: Construction worker (retired)
- Lives with spouse and adult children

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father with colon cancer at age 70.

ALLERGIES: Sulfa drugs (hives)

MEDICATIONS:
- Apixaban 5 mg BID

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: constipation, dry mouth. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 135/70, HR 87, RR 17, Temp 98.5F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 97.3 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 7.5, Hgb 12.0, Plt 259, Cr 0.85

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
60-year-old male with IV malignant melanoma of the left upper back. initial oncology consultation.

1. Malignant melanoma of the left upper back, Stage IV
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend primary resection
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Helen Park, MD
Dermatology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Kenneth Flores
MRN: patient_22786d702301
Date of Report: 2022-03-20
Specimen: Left upper back (Resection)
Diagnosis: Malignant melanoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (523 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 30.4 mutations/Mb (High)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRAF p.V600E (c.1799T>A) - Missense (Exon 15) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A Loss - Homozygous deletion
  TERT Promoter mutation (-124C>T) - Promoter

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  NF1 p.T1556M - VUS
  ARID2 p.Q1334L - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A homozygous deletion

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRAF V600E: FDA-approved BRAF/MEK inhibitors include dabrafenib/trametinib, encorafenib/binimetinib, vemurafenib/cobimetinib.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 666x coverage.
Tumor content: 73%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Kenneth Flores
MRN: patient_22786d702301
Specimen ID: S2022-41475
Date of Procedure: 2022-04-03
Date of Report: 2022-04-06

SPECIMEN: Left upper back, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Malignant melanoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 7.5 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
S-100: Positive
HMB-45: Positive
Melan-A: Positive
SOX10: Positive
Ki-67: 30%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Kenneth Flores - Left upper back" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 7.5 x 5.2 x 3.8 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A6.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Kenneth Flores
MRN: patient_22786d702301
Date of Study: 2023-01-14

CLINICAL INDICATION: Malignant melanoma of the left upper back. restaging after alternative first-line immunotherapy.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 0.7 cm in the left upper lobe. Consistent with metastatic disease.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Brain metastases as described. Recommend neurosurgical consultation.
2. Pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Kenneth Flores
MRN: patient_22786d702301
Date of Study: 2023-04-10

CLINICAL INDICATION: Malignant melanoma of the left upper back. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 2 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 0.5 cm in the left lower lobe. Stable in size.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: Lytic lesion in L2 vertebral body measuring 1.5 cm. Suspicious for metastasis.

IMPRESSION:
1. Brain metastases as described. Recommend neurosurgical consultation.
2. Pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease.
3. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology
